workflow.name: Channel Shift (3 Channels) Measurement and Track Correction

setting.value: Configure the spatial calibration of the input images, in case they are read incorrectly. Set the values to 0 to keep values from the image metadata.%%00010%%00010Configure the output directory to any local folder (default: user HOME).
Set to 0.0 to keep value from image metadata
Directory to save all output files
File list with information and configured flow variables

nodeMessage.message: Component does not have input data, execute upstream nodes first

setting.value: Table containing the list of input image files
This node reads all images from the input table, corrects spatial calibration metadata if required, and splits the channels.

nodeMessage.message: Status: Error: Match Correspondences and Measure Transform 4:981 Status: Error: Node "Fit Transformation Model to Paired Point Sets" not available from extension "KNIME Image Processing - ImageJ Integration (Beta)" (provided by "University of Konstanz"; plugin "org.knime.knip.imagej2.core" is not installed) Status: Error: Node "Fit Transformation Model to Paired Point Sets" not available from extension "KNIME Image Processing - ImageJ Integration (Beta)" (provided by "University of Konstanz"; plugin "org.knime.knip.imagej2.core" is not installed) Status: Error: Match correspondences 4:981:0:126 Status: Error: Node "Measure 3D Transformation Between Spots" not available from extension "KNIME Image Processing - ImageJ Integration (Beta)" (provided by "University of Konstanz"; plugin "org.knime.knip.imagej2.core" is not installed) Status: Error: Match correspondences 4:981:0:958 Status: Error: Node "Measure 3D Transformation Between Spots" not available from extension "KNIME Image Processing - ImageJ Integration (Beta)" (provided by "University of Konstanz"; plugin "org.knime.knip.imagej2.core" is not installed) Status: Error: Correct c2 and c3 4:981:0:171 Status: Error: Node "Apply 3D Affine Transform to Points" not available from extension "KNIME Image Processing - ImageJ Integration (Beta)" (provided by "University of Konstanz"; plugin "org.knime.knip.imagej2.core" is not installed) Status: Error: Node "Apply 3D Affine Transform to Points" not available from extension "KNIME Image Processing - ImageJ Integration (Beta)" (provided by "University of Konstanz"; plugin "org.knime.knip.imagej2.core" is not installed)
Component does not have input data, execute upstream nodes first

setting.value: This node determines the matching correspondences in the lists of coordinates from all three channels, and computes a correction model to align channels 2 and 3 with channel 1

nodeMessage.message: Component does not have input data, execute upstream nodes first

setting.value: This node generates various plots (with R/ggplot2) and saves them to the configured output folder (as .png).

nodeMessage.message: Component does not have input data, execute upstream nodes first

setting.value: library(ggplot2)%%00010library(tidyr)%%00010library(dplyr)%%00010%%00010# Multiple plot function%%00010#%%00010# ggplot objects can be passed in ..., or to plotlist (as a list of ggplot objects)%%00010# - cols: Number of columns in layout%%00010# - layout: A matrix specifying the layout. If present, 'cols' is ignored.%%00010#%%00010# If the layout is something like matrix(c(1,2,3,3), nrow=2, byrow=TRUE),%%00010# then plot 1 will go in the upper left, 2 will go in the upper right, and%%00010# 3 will go all the way across the bottom.%%00010#%%00010multiplot <- function(..., plotlist=NULL, file, cols=1, layout=NULL) {%%00010 library(grid)%%00010%%00010 # Make a list from the ... arguments and plotlist%%00010 plots <- c(list(...), plotlist)%%00010%%00010 numPlots = length(plots)%%00010%%00010 # If layout is NULL, then use 'cols' to determine layout%%00010 if (is.null(layout)) {%%00010 # Make the panel%%00010 # ncol: Number of columns of plots%%00010 # nrow: Number of rows needed, calculated from # of cols%%00010 layout <- matrix(seq(1, cols * ceiling(numPlots/cols)),%%00010 ncol = cols, nrow = ceiling(numPlots/cols))%%00010 }%%00010%%00010 if (numPlots==1) {%%00010 print(plots[[1]])%%00010%%00010 } else {%%00010 # Set up the page%%00010 grid.newpage()%%00010 pushViewport(viewport(layout = grid.layout(nrow(layout), ncol(layout))))%%00010%%00010 # Make each plot, in the correct location%%00010 for (i in 1:numPlots) {%%00010 # Get the i,j matrix positions of the regions that contain this subplot%%00010 matchidx <- as.data.frame(which(layout == i, arr.ind = TRUE))%%00010%%00010 print(plots[[i]], vp = viewport(layout.pos.row = matchidx$row,%%00010 layout.pos.col = matchidx$col))%%00010 }%%00010 }%%00010}%%00010%%00010#p <- ggplot(knime.in, aes(x=knime.in$"ix1", y=knime.in$"iy1", z=knime.in$"iz1")) +%%00010#3geom_point() +%%00010#theme_minimal()%%00010%%00010#p%%00010%%00010df <- knime.in %>%%%00010 mutate(id = rownames(knime.in)) %>% # create unique row ids%%00010 gather(i, coord, ix1:iz3) %>% # unpivot coordinates%%00010 separate(i, c("dim", "rep"), sep=2) %>% # split coordinate names%%00010 spread(dim, coord) # pivot x,y,z%%00010%%00010p1 <- ggplot(df, aes(x=df$"ix", y=df$"iy", color=df$"rep")) +%%00010geom_point(alpha=0.5) +%%00010labs(x="x", y="y") +%%00010guides(color=FALSE) +%%00010scale_color_manual(values=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7")) +%%00010theme_minimal()%%00010%%00010p2 <- ggplot(df, aes(x=df$"ix", y=df$"iz", color=df$"rep")) +%%00010geom_point(alpha=0.5) +%%00010labs(x="x", y="z") +%%00010guides(color=FALSE) +%%00010scale_color_manual(values=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7")) +%%00010theme_minimal()%%00010%%00010p3 <- ggplot(df, aes(x=df$"iz", y=df$"iy", color=df$"rep")) +%%00010geom_point(alpha=0.5) +%%00010labs(x="z", y="y") +%%00010#guides(color=FALSE) +%%00010scale_color_manual(values=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7")) +%%00010theme_minimal()%%00010%%00010multiplot(p1,p2,p3,cols=2,layout=matrix(c(1,1,1,2,1,1,1,2,1,1,1,2,3,3,3,4), nrow=4, byrow=FALSE))%%00010

setting.key: Output non numbers (NaN, Inf, -Inf) as missing cells

setting.value: library(ggplot2)%%00010library(tidyr)%%00010%%00010df <- knime.in %>% gather('d12', 'd23', 'd31', key="d", value="distance")%%00010%%00010p <- ggplot(df, aes(x=distance, group=correction, fill=correction)) +%%00010geom_density(alpha=0.8, color=NA) +%%00010facet_grid(d ~ .) +%%00010scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +%%00010scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0,0))%%00010%%00010#coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-0.5, 0.5)) +%%00010#theme_white()%%00010%%00010p%%00010

setting.key: Output non numbers (NaN, Inf, -Inf) as missing cells

setting.value: library(ggplot2)%%00010library(dplyr)%%00010library(tidyr)%%00010%%00010GeomSplitViolin <- ggproto("GeomSplitViolin", GeomViolin, %%00010 draw_group = function(self, data, ..., draw_quantiles = NULL) {%%00010 data <- transform(data, xminv = x - violinwidth * (x - xmin), xmaxv = x + violinwidth * (xmax - x))%%00010 grp <- data[1, "group"]%%00010# newdata <- plyr::arrange(transform(data, x = if (grp %%00037% 2 == 1) xminv else xmaxv), if (grp %%00037% 2 == 1) y else -y)%%00010 newdata <- plyr::arrange(transform(data, x = xmaxv), y)%%00010 newdata <- rbind(newdata[1, ], newdata, newdata[nrow(newdata), ], newdata[1, ])%%00010 newdata[c(1, nrow(newdata) - 1, nrow(newdata)), "x"] <- round(newdata[1, "x"])%%00010%%00010 if (length(draw_quantiles) > 0 & !scales::zero_range(range(data$y))) {%%00010 stopifnot(all(draw_quantiles >= 0), all(draw_quantiles <=%%00010 1))%%00010 quantiles <- ggplot2:::create_quantile_segment_frame(data, draw_quantiles)%%00010 aesthetics <- data[rep(1, nrow(quantiles)), setdiff(names(data), c("x", "y")), drop = FALSE]%%00010 aesthetics$alpha <- rep(1, nrow(quantiles))%%00010 both <- cbind(quantiles, aesthetics)%%00010 quantile_grob <- GeomPath$draw_panel(both, ...)%%00010 ggplot2:::ggname("geom_split_violin", grid::grobTree(GeomPolygon$draw_panel(newdata, ...), quantile_grob))%%00010 }%%00010 else {%%00010 ggplot2:::ggname("geom_split_violin", GeomPolygon$draw_panel(newdata, ...))%%00010 }%%00010})%%00010%%00010geom_split_violin <- function(mapping = NULL, data = NULL, stat = "ydensity", position = "identity", ..., %%00010 draw_quantiles = NULL, trim = TRUE, scale = "area", na.rm = FALSE, %%00010 show.legend = NA, inherit.aes = TRUE) {%%00010 layer(data = data, mapping = mapping, stat = stat, geom = GeomSplitViolin, %%00010 position = position, show.legend = show.legend, inherit.aes = inherit.aes, %%00010 params = list(trim = trim, scale = scale, draw_quantiles = draw_quantiles, na.rm = na.rm, ...))%%00010}%%00010%%00010df <- knime.in %>% gather('dx12', 'dy12', 'dz12', 'dx23', 'dy23', 'dz23', 'dx31', 'dy31', 'dz31', key="d", value="distance")%%00010%%00010#df$correction <- factor(df$correction, c("after", "before"))%%00010df$d <- factor(df$d, c('dx12', 'dy12', 'dz12', 'dx23', 'dy23', 'dz23', 'dx31', 'dy31', 'dz31'))%%00010%%00010p <- ggplot(df, aes(x=d, y=distance, group=interaction(d,correction), fill=factor(correction))) +%%00010geom_split_violin(alpha=0.5, trim=FALSE, color=NA) +%%00010#geom_violin() +%%00010coord_cartesian(ylim=c(-0.5, 0.5)) +%%00010theme_minimal()%%00010%%00010p%%00010

setting.key: Output non numbers (NaN, Inf, -Inf) as missing cells

setting.value: library(ggplot2)%%00010library(tidyr)%%00010library(dplyr)%%00010%%00010# Multiple plot function%%00010#%%00010# ggplot objects can be passed in ..., or to plotlist (as a list of ggplot objects)%%00010# - cols: Number of columns in layout%%00010# - layout: A matrix specifying the layout. If present, 'cols' is ignored.%%00010#%%00010# If the layout is something like matrix(c(1,2,3,3), nrow=2, byrow=TRUE),%%00010# then plot 1 will go in the upper left, 2 will go in the upper right, and%%00010# 3 will go all the way across the bottom.%%00010#%%00010multiplot <- function(..., plotlist=NULL, file, cols=1, layout=NULL) {%%00010 library(grid)%%00010%%00010 # Make a list from the ... arguments and plotlist%%00010 plots <- c(list(...), plotlist)%%00010%%00010 numPlots = length(plots)%%00010%%00010 # If layout is NULL, then use 'cols' to determine layout%%00010 if (is.null(layout)) {%%00010 # Make the panel%%00010 # ncol: Number of columns of plots%%00010 # nrow: Number of rows needed, calculated from # of cols%%00010 layout <- matrix(seq(1, cols * ceiling(numPlots/cols)),%%00010 ncol = cols, nrow = ceiling(numPlots/cols))%%00010 }%%00010%%00010 if (numPlots==1) {%%00010 print(plots[[1]])%%00010%%00010 } else {%%00010 # Set up the page%%00010 grid.newpage()%%00010 pushViewport(viewport(layout = grid.layout(nrow(layout), ncol(layout))))%%00010%%00010 # Make each plot, in the correct location%%00010 for (i in 1:numPlots) {%%00010 # Get the i,j matrix positions of the regions that contain this subplot%%00010 matchidx <- as.data.frame(which(layout == i, arr.ind = TRUE))%%00010%%00010 print(plots[[i]], vp = viewport(layout.pos.row = matchidx$row,%%00010 layout.pos.col = matchidx$col))%%00010 }%%00010 }%%00010}%%00010%%00010#p <- ggplot(knime.in, aes(x=knime.in$"ix1", y=knime.in$"iy1", z=knime.in$"iz1")) +%%00010#3geom_point() +%%00010#theme_minimal()%%00010%%00010#p%%00010%%00010df <- knime.in %>%%%00010 mutate(id = rownames(knime.in)) %>% # create unique row ids%%00010 gather(i, coord, ix1:iz3) %>% # unpivot coordinates%%00010 separate(i, c("dim", "rep"), sep=2) %>% # split coordinate names%%00010 spread(dim, coord) # pivot x,y,z%%00010%%00010p1 <- ggplot(df, aes(x=df$"ix", y=df$"iy", color=df$"rep")) +%%00010geom_point(alpha=0.5) +%%00010labs(x="x", y="y") +%%00010guides(color=FALSE) +%%00010scale_color_manual(values=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7")) +%%00010theme_minimal()%%00010%%00010p2 <- ggplot(df, aes(x=df$"ix", y=df$"iz", color=df$"rep")) +%%00010geom_point(alpha=0.5) +%%00010labs(x="x", y="z") +%%00010guides(color=FALSE) +%%00010scale_color_manual(values=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7")) +%%00010theme_minimal()%%00010%%00010p3 <- ggplot(df, aes(x=df$"iz", y=df$"iy", color=df$"rep")) +%%00010geom_point(alpha=0.5) +%%00010labs(x="z", y="y") +%%00010#guides(color=FALSE) +%%00010scale_color_manual(values=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7")) +%%00010theme_minimal()%%00010%%00010multiplot(p1,p2,p3,cols=2,layout=matrix(c(1,1,1,2,1,1,1,2,1,1,1,2,3,3,3,4), nrow=4, byrow=FALSE))%%00010

setting.key: Output non numbers (NaN, Inf, -Inf) as missing cells

node.name: GroupBy
node.annotation: Compute MSE per bead image (before and after correction)

nodeMessage.message: Component does not have input data, execute upstream nodes first

annotations.annotation:
Channel Shift Measurement and Track Correction
This workflow was created for the research group of Luca Giorgetti by Jan Eglinger, Friedrich Miescher Institute for Biomedical Research (FMI), Basel
In order to use this workflow, please make sure you installed the following extensions:
- FMI KNIME Plugins from the FMI update site: https://community.knime.org/download/ch.fmi.knime.plugins.update/
- KNIME Image Processing - ImageJ Integration - KNIME Interactive R Statistics Integration - KNIME Nodes for Wide Data (many columns)
Note for Mac OSX users: to select a directory in the file chooser dialog (when configuring the output folder), navigate to the parent folder, then select the target folder (but don't double-click) and click Choose.

Quality control:
Plot distances between bead positions
Choose input folder for bead images
Adjust detection parameters for each channel if necessary
Choose input folder for trajectories (xml files)
Choose output folder for corrected xml
Configure pixel spacing and output location

metadata.author: Jan Eglinger
metadata.description:
Channel Shift (3 Channels) Measurement and Track Correction
This workflow measures channel shift (caused by chromatic aberration and/or camera misalignment) from 3-channel images of fluorescent beads, and applies the measured transformation to correct track coordinates.
It requires the ‘FMI KNIME Plugins’ extension from the following update site: https://community.knime.org/download/ch.fmi.knime.plugins.update/
TAGS: chromatic-aberration,channel-shift-correction,imagej
